title: Model-Assisted Teleoperation
description: Keep in Survey: model-assisted teleop is still a plausible project, but it needs one concrete operator-assist loop instead of a broad list of video-model ideas.
summary: Survey-stage track extracted from the Notion ideas page: turn teleoperation from direct human control into a model-assisted loop where a world model predicts likely futures, exposes contact/object risk, and uses low-cost UMI or rendered data to scale demonstrations without immediately becoming another generic video-generation project.

details.text: Core Notion idea: Model-assisted Teleoperation = text prompter -> world prompter, where an agentic R2S2R / controllable VideoGen / Image-Layered world model previews outcomes before or during teleop. This is not yet a standalone dashboard project; it should sit in survey until a concrete demo is chosen.

TelePhantom-new: use a 3D URDF-conditioned subject inside video generation so qpos / future object state can generate a phantom preview. This is the most direct continuation of TelePreview, but needs a measurable criterion: preview alignment, collision/contact warning, or faster operator correction.

UMI2Render2Real: combine Real2Render2Real, differentiable robot rendering, Masquerade-style robotization, UMIGS / Real2Sim evaluation, and UMI data to generate semi-teleop or robot-agnostic data. This overlaps with the active UMI card, but the assisted-teleop framing is still unclaimed and should remain survey until it has its own protocol.

Low-cost UMI: webcam + IMU only, fiducial objects for tracking, and possible camera-depth-model augmentation. This is promising only if it can preserve action recoverability and calibration quality; otherwise it is just a cheaper recorder with weaker labels.

Video-to-state sub-idea: infer robot joint values, camera pose, and articulated object state from videos using URDF, stereo/depth, VGGT, point tracking, and articulated pose estimation. This can become the state-estimation layer for model-assisted teleop, but the missing hard piece is robust articulated object pose under contact.

details: Other open sub-ideas from Notion: explicit contact annotations or arrows/fields in video models, panorama generation followed by VR video, automatic keyframe/progress/corner-case evaluation, and diffusion-forcing style VLA/video rollout. Keep them as methods inside this survey rather than separate dashboard cards.

details.text: Notion migration addendum 2026-05-31: also folded in low-cost UMI webcam+IMU+fiducial tracking, camera-depth augmentation, UMI2Render2Real / Real2Render2Real, explicit contact arrows or fields in video models, panorama-to-VR video, and automatic video2sim reward / keyframe / corner-case evaluation. These remain methods inside one assisted-teleop card.

ICRA 2026 poster update: Foundational World Models Accurately Detect Bimanual Manipulator Failures is directly relevant because it turns model assistance into a runtime failure/risk monitor instead of requiring a photorealistic future preview. It trains a small probabilistic world model in NVIDIA Cosmos Tokenizer latent space on nominal bimanual teleop data, then uses world-model uncertainty / prediction error with conformal thresholds to flag cable-drop failures. For our card, this suggests the first measurable loop should be an overlayed uncertainty gauge or stop/correct signal during teleop.

risks_decisions: Do not duplicate the active UMI / Image-Layered World Model card. This survey must answer the teleoperation question: does prediction or preview measurably improve operator speed, safety, or correction rate?
Rendering-only data generation is not enough; the task needs object/contact state, action faithfulness, and a policy/evaluation bridge.
Low-cost UMI is only worth keeping if camera/IMU/fiducial labels can recover action and calibration with acceptable error.
First demo should be small: one contact-rich teleop task with preview on/off, not a full general world-model platform.

Survey disposition 2026-05-31: keep, because no single reference closes the full loop of teleop preview, contact risk, operator correction, and measurable data-quality improvement for our UMI/Franka/ARX setting.

Next executable analysis: choose one offline preview experiment on a recorded clip, define inputs/outputs, and score whether the preview changes operator decisions or flags contact/object-state failures.
Fold video-to-state, contact arrows/fields, and panorama-to-VR into this card as methods; do not create separate dashboard cards until one method has a measurable demo.

Ideas migrated here rather than new cards: low-cost UMI, video-to-state, UMI2Render2Real, contact-field video annotation, panorama-to-VR, Diffusion-Forcing VLA rollout, and embodied-video evaluation. Promote only after one method has a measurable preview/correction demo.

Research update 2026-06-05: the Stanford/Watney Cosmos-latent failure monitor suggests a narrower first demo than full video preview: train on nominal teleop episodes and show an uncertainty / nonconformity warning when the operator approaches an out-of-distribution contact or object-state failure.
Do not claim assistance unless it changes an operator-facing decision: measure warning lead time, false alarm rate, correction rate, and task completion delta with monitor on/off.
asset_alt: Model-assisted teleoperation loop with world-model preview and risk overlay
asset_caption: Survey diagram: operator input, world-model preview, contact-risk overlay, and corrected teleoperation output.
